back: The craft of building systems that collect, store, clean, organize, and serve data so it becomes useful — at scale and at speed.
Information in its original, unprocessed form: messy, inconsistent, and not yet ready to trust or use.
Not storing data (that's cheap now), but turning huge amounts of raw data into something reliable and useful.
Where data comes from — clicks, purchases, sensor readings, app logs, and customer interactions.

front: Why storage stopped being the bottleneck
back: Cloud storage became cheap enough to keep almost everything, so the hard part moved to making sense of the data.
One trusted, shared version of the data, so separate copies don't drift apart and contradict each other.
As data grows from thousands to billions of records, tools that once worked fine can slow down or break entirely.
Some questions can wait for overnight reports; others (fraud, live recommendations) need answers in seconds or real time.
